positiveClaims: The existing release claim packet emit gate is product-bound as a bounded internal Review Bridge 12F packet boundary.
Accepted 12F requires canonical packetMeta with packetId, createdAtUtc, and emitterId only.
Accepted 12F requires accepted 12D release claim mode decision provenance and re-evaluates raw 12D decision input before accepting.
Accepted 12F requires accepted 12E release claim attestation provenance and re-evaluates raw 12E attestation input before accepting.
Accepted 12F requires 12D and 12E mode, claimId, dossierId, matrixId, and decisionHash bindings to match.
Accepted 12F emits a deterministic release claim packet and strict report with fixed schemas and fixed strict report keys.
The release claim packet hash is stable and excludes createdAtUtc from the hash body.
RELEASE_MODE packet modeClass is USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY only as an internal packet/report class and does not create user-facing release authority.
The 12F packet emit runtime now strips inherited prototype fields before packetMeta, 12D, and 12E envelope acceptance checks.
This contour is packet emit binding truth only; it is not release execution, release publication, user-facing release, import, export, apply, storage truth, or release readiness truth.
nonClaims: No release readiness is claimed.
No user-facing release is claimed.
No release execution completion is claimed.
No release publication completion is claimed.
No release-wide publication authority is claimed.
No Word support is claimed.
No Google Docs support is claimed.
No DOCX support expansion is claimed.
No import support is claimed.
No export support is claimed.
No review import completion is claimed.
No roundtrip is claimed.
No layout parity is claimed.
No full fidelity is claimed.
No automatic apply is claimed.
No structural apply is claimed.
No comment auto-apply is claimed.
No manuscript write is performed by release claim packet emit binding.
No project truth write is performed by release claim packet emit binding.
No receipt or recovery evidence is created by release claim packet emit binding.
No DOCX import safe-create behavior is changed.
No DOCX export behavior is changed.
No import/export MVP closeout is widened.
No PDF, EPUB, HTML, Mindmap, cloud sync, cross-platform readiness, packaged readiness, or Y9 admission is claimed.
layerDecisions: Packet emit binding is internal release-claim packet truth only.
Packet emit acceptance does not become project truth by itself.
Packet emit acceptance does not write manuscript truth or project truth.
Packet emit acceptance does not admit release execution or release publication.
Packet emit acceptance does not prove full fidelity, layout parity, roundtrip, import correctness, or export correctness.
USER_FACING_CLAIM_READY is an internal packet modeClass only and is not a user-facing UI or publication state.
This contour is not a user-facing boundary contour.
This contour is not a release execution contour.
This contour is not a release publication contour.
Packet emit binding is not a parser and does not inspect external documents.
Packet emit binding is not content import and does not create scenes.
Packet emit binding is not Review import automation and does not activate a Review session.
Packet emit binding is not an apply plan and does not authorize mutation.
Packet emit binding is not safe write and does not create receipt or recovery evidence.
Packet emit binding is not export and does not alter DOCX output.
Review Bridge may reference accepted 12F output as bounded internal chain evidence, but accepted 12F output does not become project truth.

machineChecks.command: node --test release claim attestation and packet emit baseline contracts
node --test release claim packet emit contracts after inherited-property hardening
node --test release claim packet emit binding targeted contracts
node --test release claim packet emit binding plus adjacent 12B through 12F contracts
node --test release claim full contract pack

agentAudits.finding: Confirmed 12F is the logical next contour only as bounded packet emit binding over existing 12F kernel truth, with mandatory nonclaims against release readiness, user-facing release, execution, publication, import/export, apply, storage, receipt, recovery, and Y9.

Identified inherited-property bypass cases where Object.create accepted 12D or 12E envelopes and prototype packetMeta could be accepted; resolved by own-enumerable JSON normalization in 12F and explicit inherited-property negative tests.

Initially found status self-contradiction between scope.runtimeProductionCodeChanged true and implementation.changedProductionRuntime false; resolved by setting implementation.changedProductionRuntime true, binding it in contract, and revalidating approvals, governance, and targeted tests.

nextStep: after delivery, select the next owner-approved boundary explicitly; release execution, release publication, and post-MVP import/export expansion do not open by implication
